title: Uniforms
wikitext:
[]: Uniforms             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Dec 26, 2007 4:31 pm What are these dresses called? There's something similar called a pinafore, but those are different in that there's material covering the entire torso.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Dec 26, 2007 8:40 pm I guess this is a variation on the Seira Fuku. The collar isn't as large as it is on the other Seira Fuku. (Seira Fuku- Seira for Sailor. Japanese school uniforms are loosely based on Nineteenth and Early 20th century military uniforms) Rei is wearing a double-breasted skirt over the shirt. Most of the uniforms seen- for instance, take a look at Kagome in Inuyasha and Hitomi in Escaflowne- do not have those straps going over the back and the chest. As a matter of fact, in a Japan that is in perpetual summer, it would be ridiculously hot. My sister had a skirt like that for her school uniform a few years ago, and she was always complaining about how hot it was. I wonder why Shinji needs the undershirt, for that matter. It would be HOT with that on.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 26, 2007 9:44 pm UrsusArctos wrote: I wonder why Shinji needs the undershirt, for that matter. It would be HOT with that on.

[]:             Quote Post by Avatar of Dreams » Thu Dec 27, 2007 12:01 am UrsusArctos wrote: I wonder why Shinji needs the undershirt, for that matter. It would be HOT with that on.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Dec 27, 2007 12:11 am He does wear a uniform once. School jock? Or athlete? Well. (1000th post. Oh God.)

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Dec 27, 2007 10:11 am UrsusArctos wrote: I guess this is a variation on the Seira Fuku. The collar isn't as large as it is on the other Seira Fuku. (Seira Fuku- Seira for Sailor. Japanese school uniforms are loosely based on Nineteenth and Early 20th century military uniforms)

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Dec 27, 2007 5:07 pm I think it is the sailour uniform, that has been simplified around the collar, as the great big square thing would just be hot (added to the already thick garments they have to wear). It is a pinafore, yes, but has a open front, and is missing the big thing that should be covering the legs (probably compensated by the skirt). When you mean by a underskirt, I think in this case, it is a petticoat. Regarding Shinji's uniform, it resembles the current Maritime Self Defence Force's service uniform, minust the matching white trousers, eapulettes, cap, and black shoes. Yes, school uniforms were kind of replications of WW2. Eg.: For boys, the tsumeiri and the cap is basically the complete imitation of the winter uniform for Naval Officer cadets, minus the epaulettes, and the dagger.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Dec 27, 2007 5:22 pm The Imperialist wrote: Yes, school uniforms were kind of replications of WW2. Eg.: For boys, the tsumeiri and the cap is basically the complete imitation of the winter uniform for Naval Officer cadets, minus the epaulettes, and the dagger.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Dec 27, 2007 5:40 pm Well, the Navy copied, then made modifications, and the schools just decided to catch on with it. It has no correlation with the army uniforms. I know it was mostly after the second World War, as before (and during the second world war), it was more towards the hakama and the lot, but with a cap.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Dec 27, 2007 7:41 pm Sorry about the goofup, Zugz. However, that underskirt is a part of the big skirt itself, or I'm very mistaken. Rei doesn't wear a petticoat. And a sailor collar wouldn't do for that pinafore, anyway...notice that the straps are too high up and would have the collar tucked inwards. Shinji's normal uniform looks like the school uniforms I've seen in India, there's nothing special about it (Apart from the fact that his undershirt is always blue). The winter uniform, like the Imperialist said, looks military-based. High collar and everything.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Dec 28, 2007 10:32 am UrsusArctos wrote: However, that underskirt is a part of the big skirt itself, or I'm very mistaken. Rei doesn't wear a petticoat.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Dec 28, 2007 11:21 am I mean, having a big square of material would be clumsy to tuck in, since you're wearing the pinafore on top of the shirt. The collar is meant to hang out, which is fine if it were just the shirt and a skirt. But with a pinafore, it would be troublesome to keep the square of material out, which is why it isn't there.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Dec 28, 2007 5:18 pm It maybe like you know, a copy of a Dirndl, but with a open front, instead of being buttoned. If it isn't a petticoat (but it is too frilly to be something else!) then it is a frilly version of a half slip, that is for some reason attached to the skirt itself. I am just saying Shinji's uniform resembles the MSDF service uniform, because of its collar.

[]:             Quote Post by RetroRainbow » Sat Dec 29, 2007 12:05 pm Zugzwang wrote: UrsusArctos wrote: However, that underskirt is a part of the big skirt itself, or I'm very mistaken. Rei doesn't wear a petticoat.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:42 am Well, it is either a half slip, or a petticoat.
summary:
[]: Uniforms             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Dec 26, 2007 4:31 pm What are these dresses called? There's something similar called a pinafore, but those are different in that there's material covering the entire torso.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Dec 26, 2007 8:40 pm I guess this is a variation on the Seira Fuku. The collar isn't as large as it is
